zh.lifeSummary: 當James S. Nute於1613年出生在英國爾迪維頓時，他的父親James Newte39歲，母親Agnes Burrowe37歲。他於1638年與Sarah Heard在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加劍橋大學默里 哈普爾斯特拉福德多福結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他去世於1691年在美國新罕布夏州斯特拉福德多福 內可享年78歲，埋葬在美國新罕布夏州斯特拉福德。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当James S. Nute于1613年出生在英国爾迪維頓时，他的父亲James Newte39岁，母亲Agnes Burrowe37岁。他于1638年与Sarah Heard在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加劍橋大學默里 哈普爾斯特拉福德多福结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和4个女儿的父母。他去世于1691年在美国新罕布什尔州斯特拉福德多福 內可享年78岁，埋葬在美国新罕布什尔州斯特拉福德。

nameMeanings.name: Nute
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Cornwall): probably a variant of   Newitt  .

variants.name: Newitt
meanings: English (Northamptonshire): from Middle English newete, the older, unshortened, form of newte ‘newt’, itself for Old English efete with initial n detached from a preceding indefinite article.

nameMeanings.name: James
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the name borne in the New Testament by two of Christ's disciples, James son of Zebedee and James son of Alphaeus. This form comes from Late Latin Iacomus, a variant of Iacobus, Latin form of Greek Iakobos. This is the same name as Old Testament Jacob (Hebrew Yaakov), but for many centuries now they have been thought of in the English-speaking world as two distinct names. In Britain, James is a royal name that from the beginning of the 15th century onwards was associated particularly with the Scottish house of Stewart: James I of Scotland ( 1394–1437 ; ruled 1424–37 ) was a patron of the arts and a noted poet, as well as an energetic ruler. King James VI of Scotland ( 1566–1625 ; reigned 1567–1625 ) succeeded to the throne of England in 1603 . His grandson, James II of England ( 1633–1701 ; reigned 1685–8 ) was a Roman Catholic, deposed in 1688 in favour of his Protestant daughter Mary and her husband William of Orange. From then on he, his son (also called James), and his grandson Charles (‘Bonnie Prince Charlie’) made various unsuccessful attempts to recover the English throne. Their supporters were known as Jacobites (from Latin Iacobus), and the name James became for a while particularly associated with Roman Catholicism on the one hand, and Highland opposition to the English government on the other. Nevertheless, it has since become one of the most perennially popular boys' names.

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

historicEvents.title: Glorious Revolution
historicEvents.description: The Glorious Revolution brought the downfall of Catholic King James II and the reign of his Protestant daughter Mary and her Dutch husband, William III of Orange.

en.lifeSummary: When James S. Nute was born in 1613, in Tiverton, Cheshire, England, his father, James Newte, was 39 and his mother, Agnes Burrowe, was 37. He married Sarah Heard in 1638, in Dover, Strafford, New Hampshire, British Colonial America. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 4 daughters. He died in 1691, in Dover Neck, Strafford, New Hampshire, United States, at the age of 78, and was buried in Strafford, New Hampshire, United States.

Il a épousé Sarah Heard en 1638, à Dover, Strafford, New Hampshire, Amérique coloniale britannique. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 4 filles. Lui sposato Sarah Heard nel 1638, nel Dover, Strafford, New Hampshire, British Colonial America. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 4 figlie. ja.lifeSummary: James S. Nuteが1613年にイギリスチェシュアイアティバートンで生まれた時，父James Newteは39歳，母Agnes Burroweは37歳であった。1638年にイギリス植民地アメリカニュー ハンプシュアイアストラフォードドバーでSarah Heardと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼は1691年にアメリカ合衆国ニューハンプシャー州ストラフォードドバー ネックで78歳で死去し，アメリカ合衆国ニューハンプシャー州ストラフォードにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: James S. Nute(이)가 1613년 영국 체실르 티버턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 James Newte(은)는 39세였고, 그의 어머니 Agnes Burrowe(은)는 37세였다. 그는 1638년 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 뉴 함프실르 스트래퍼드 도버에서 Sarah Heard(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1691년 미국 뉴햄프셔 주 스트래퍼드 도버 니c크에서 78세의 나이로 사망했으며 미국 뉴햄프셔 주 스트래퍼드에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando James S. Nute nasceu em 1613, em Tiverton, Cheshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, James Newte, tinha 39 anos e sua mãe, Agnes Burrowe, tinha 37 anos. Ele casou-se com Sarah Heard em 1638, em Dover, Strafford, New Hampshire, British Colonial America. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 4 filhas. Ele faleceu em 1691, em Dover Neck, Strafford, Nova Hampshire, Estados Unidos da América, com 78 anos, e foi sepultado em Strafford, Nova Hampshire, Estados Unidos da América.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда James S. Nute родился в 1613 г. в местности тивиртон, чешир, Англия, его отцу по имени James Newte было 39 лет, а матери, которую звали Agnes Burrowe, 37. Он женился на женщине по имени Sarah Heard в 1638 г. в местности довир, страффорд, ню хампшир, бритиш колоняль амирика. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 4 дочери. Он умер в 1691 г. в местности довир некк, страффорд, Нью-Гэмпшир, Соединенные штаты в возрасте 78 лет и был похоронен в местности страффорд, Нью-Гэмпшир, Соединенные штаты.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando James S. Nute nació en 1613, en Tiverton, Cheshire, Inglaterra, su papá, James Newte, tenía 39 años y su mamá, Agnes Burrowe, tenía 37 años. Se casó con Sarah Heard en 1638, en Dover, Strafford, New Hampshire, British Colonial America. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 4 hijas. Murió en 1691, en Dover Neck, Strafford, Nuevo Hampshire, Estados Unidos, a la edad de 78 años, y fue sepultado en Strafford, Nuevo Hampshire, Estados Unidos.
